claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a post-execution boundary detection mechanism (not prevention) that allows Write tools but detects project file mutations via SHA256 snapshot diff, with unit test coverage confirming detection after mutation, but stress tests show no actual mutations from real Claude agents.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates SHA256 hash map of all project files, excluding allowed write roots
diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files

evidence.why: Proves boundary mode allows write mutations
Settings prove agents have write permissions even in boundary mode

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot taken BEFORE agent execution loop
evidence.why: Proves detection is post-execution, not prevention

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter snapshot taken AFTER all agents complete, diff compared, boundary.violated set if changes found
evidence.why: Proves mutations detected after execution, not prevented during

evidence.summary: BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers created after boundary diff, PRD readiness set to false if mutations detected
evidence.why: Shows blockers are added after mutations already occurred

evidence.summary: Unit test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' uses fake agent that writes unexpected-project-write.txt during execution, then confirms detection and blocker added
evidence.why: Synthetic test proves boundary mechanism CAN detect mutations after they occur

evidence.summary: Unit test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' confirms detection of untracked file additions
evidence.why: Proves boundary works with git repositories and detects untracked mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'claude dispatch keeps tools available and relies on boundary enforcement' confirms Read, Bash, WebSearch tools allowed and Write tools available in boundary profile
evidence.why: Confirms tool permissions in boundary mode

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization for full semantic mode' shows 'full' profile requires allowFullAgentTools=true and includes Write tools
evidence.why: Proves full-tool agents have write access when authorized

evidence.summary: Stress test ledger shows 4 iterations with real Claude agents, all with boundary='clean', no actual mutations occurred during stress testing
evidence.why: Real-world evidence shows agents don't mutate in practice, but doesn't prove they can't

evidence.summary: Git status shows 55 modified files and 15 new untracked files including demand evidence system files, confirming dirty working tree
evidence.why: Confirms demand claim about dirty working tree state

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with explicit 'writes_code: false' field
evidence.why: Shows protocol specification claims agents don't write, but tool permissions allow it

evidence.summary: Claude provider configuration sets claude_tools='default', claude_allowed_tools=CLAUD_BOUNDARY_TOOLS (including Write), claude_disallowed_tools=''
evidence.why: Confirms all mutation tools are passed to Claude CLI

assumptions: Claude Code CLI honors claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools flags to restrict tool availability
Agent tool profile 'boundary' is intended to mean read-only but implementation actually allows write tools
Real Claude agents in stress tests did not attempt to write project files, but absence of evidence is not evidence of absence
Post-execution boundary detection is sufficient to prevent PRD readiness when mutations occur
Unit tests with fake agents accurately represent real Claude agent behavior
risks: Write tools are available to full-tool Claude agents during evidence gathering, allowing potential project mutations before detection
Boundary detection is post-execution (after mutations already occurred) not prevention (before mutations happen)
No stress test evidence shows real Claude agents attempting and failing to mutate due to boundary restrictions
Protocol specifies writes_code=false but tool permissions allow Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit, creating contract ambiguity
If agent mutates project files early in execution and later agent results pass, PRD readiness could incorrectly succeed if boundary logic has bugs
missing: Evidence from real Claude agent stress tests where agents actually attempted to mutate project files and were prevented
Evidence that Claude Code CLI honors claude_allowed_tools/claude_disallowed_tools flags at runtime
Evidence that boundary mechanism works correctly when agents use Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tools (not just Write)
Evidence that boundary detection catches mutations before PRD readiness is evaluated
Evidence of any preventive boundary mechanism (tool permission enforcement) vs only post-hoc detection
Evidence that agent tool profile 'full' with allowFullAgentTools=true is required for agents to actually mutate files

result.notes: Core claim verified: boundary mechanism exists and is implemented with snapshot diff detection. However, critical nuance: boundary is post-execution detection, not prevention, and Write tools are allowed even in 'boundary' mode. Stress tests show no mutations from real agents, but unit tests use synthetic mutations. Cross-check recommended to verify tool permission enforcement and prevention vs detection semantics.
